title: AI Agent Design Patterns
frameworks: Operating Model: Humans decide strategy, agents execute tactics
CRM Contact Lifecycle (prospect → lead → customer → alumni)
Agent Behavior Contract (inputs, outputs, side effects, invariants)
concepts: Agents must have explicit behavior contracts defining I/O and side effects
An ops agent needs 15-20 tools for real production work
Agent evaluations should cover correctness, boundary respect, and governance compliance
Human-agent boundary: humans own strategy and escalation; agents own repetitive execution
Onboarding workflow guides new entities through a structured question sequence
Contact lifecycle tracks entities through states: prospect, lead, active, alumni
Agent communication must be orchestrator-mediated, never direct peer-to-peer
Each agent owns an isolated memory namespace with no cross-contamination
context: Extracted from ordostudio — production agent patterns including an intake agent with structured question flows, a maestro ops agent with 18 registered tools and 14 evaluation criteria, and a CRM-integrated contact lifecycle. The operating model strictly separates human strategic decisions from agent tactical execution.
